recommendations.dependencies: Existing AppleScript bridge for Notes write; EventKit for Reminders.
recommendations.risks: AppleScript write is brittle across Notes versions; gate behind a clear opt-in toggle in IntegrationsView.

recommendations.title: Make 'Private on-device AI' a provable, native-positioned feature
recommendations.description: Add a Privacy/AI panel (extend SettingsView/IntegrationsView) that states, with live values, which engine is running ('Apple on-device model' or 'Ollama local'), '0 bytes left this Mac', audio retention window, and vault path. Contrast explicitly with cloud notetakers. Tie this to the StoreKitManager Pro gate (currently a stub with no real StoreKit calls): the differentiator a privacy-conscious buyer pays for is provable locality, not feature count.
recommendations.userValue: Turns the invisible local-first moat into the explicit, legible reason to choose (and pay for) MeetingScribe over Granola/Otter — directly answering the 2026 bot-backlash and Recall/Limitless trust collapse.
recommendations.noveltyNote: Reinforces G5_comp_notetakers C1-5 (privacy posture) but adds the Apple-native angle: surfacing the on-device engine identity (Apple vs Ollama) as the proof, and tying it to the dormant StoreKit Pro gate.
recommendations.dependencies: Reads engine state from the new LLMProvider; honest retention values.
recommendations.risks: Must not over-claim — only assert locality the code actually guarantees.

recommendations.title: Continuity / Handoff so an open meeting follows the user across devices
recommendations.description: No NSUserActivity/Handoff anywhere. Donate an NSUserActivity (isEligibleForHandoff/Search/Prediction) for the currently-open meeting and the 'record' action, carrying the meetingscribe:// URL. Combined with the iCloud inbox already in MeetingScribeApp.wireInboxWatcher (iPhone Shortcuts → vault), this lets a meeting started/viewed on the Mac be handed to a future iOS companion and makes the activity Siri-suggestable.
recommendations.userValue: The current meeting is continuable on other Apple devices and surfaces as a Siri Suggestion / on the lock screen — the ecosystem glue power users expect.
recommendations.noveltyNote: Net-new: Continuity/Handoff is entirely absent and unmentioned in every prior plan.
recommendations.dependencies: Stable meetingscribe:// identifiers (already exist); useful mainly once a companion app/iOS target exists.
recommendations.risks: Limited payoff until there is a second device target; cheap to add as scaffolding now.

recommendations.title: Expose the MCP vault to Apple Intelligence via App Intents (not just Claude)
recommendations.description: The vault is queryable by Claude through MeetingScribeMCP (17 tools) but is invisible to Apple Intelligence / Siri because the read/write operations aren't exposed as App Intents. Re-expose the highest-value MCP operations (search vault, get person, list action items due, log encounter, add memory) as App Intents backed by the same VaultCommandWatcher/SecondBrainStore layer the MCP server uses, so Siri/Spotlight/Shortcuts can drive the second-brain with the same verbs Claude already can.
recommendations.userValue: 'Hey Siri, what are my open action items for the Acme project?' answered from the local vault — the second-brain becomes reachable by the assistant already on the device, not only by Claude Desktop.
recommendations.noveltyNote: Net-new framing: prior MCP docs (C4/C5) focus on the Claude/Granola-style external-AI surface; none propose mirroring those tools as App Intents so Apple's own assistant can use them.
recommendations.dependencies: Refactor MCP tool handlers to share a vault-command core with App Intents; the App Intent suite item.
recommendations.risks: Two front-ends to one command layer — keep the command core single-sourced to avoid drift.

recommendations.title: Use NaturalLanguage for on-device entities, topics, and sentiment (no LLM cost)
recommendations.description: PersonExtractor and action-item extraction lean on the LLM; NaturalLanguage (NLTagger/NLEmbedding) is unused (0 imports). Add a cheap on-device NL pass on transcripts: NLTagger named-entity recognition to seed person/org candidates before the LLM call, NLEmbedding for topic clustering on the transcript timeline, and the built-in sentiment score per segment — feeding the conversation-intelligence panel competitors gate behind paid tiers.
recommendations.userValue: Faster, cheaper, fully-local entity/topic/sentiment extraction that improves people detection and unlocks a talk-topic timeline without burning LLM tokens or Ollama time.
recommendations.noveltyNote: Net-new substrate: G5 mentions diarization-driven conversation intelligence, but using Apple's NaturalLanguage framework as the cheap on-device NER/topic/sentiment engine is unproposed.
recommendations.dependencies: Feeds PersonExtractionController and the (planned) conversation-intelligence panel.
recommendations.risks: NL NER is coarser than an LLM; use it to seed/cheapen, not replace, the LLM extraction.

recommendations.title: Apple Intelligence Writing Tools surface for summaries and follow-up drafts
recommendations.description: Meeting summaries, Notes-tab editing, and the follow-up email draft are LLM-generated but offer no native rewrite affordances. On Tahoe, standard text views get Writing Tools (rewrite/proofread/make-concise/change-tone) for free, and Foundation Models can power custom in-context rewrites. Ensure the summary/notes/draft editors (UnifiedMeetingDetail Notes/Summary tabs, follow-up composer) use editable text surfaces that expose Writing Tools, and add a 'Rewrite tone' control for follow-up emails backed by Foundation Models.
recommendations.userValue: Polish a summary or retune a follow-up email's tone with the system Writing Tools users already know, in-place — no copy-paste to another app.
recommendations.noveltyNote: Net-new: no prior doc mentions Apple Writing Tools / system rewrite affordances for the generated text surfaces.
recommendations.dependencies: Editable NSTextView/TextEditor surfaces; optional Foundation Models for custom rewrites.
recommendations.risks: Writing Tools availability is OS/region-gated; degrade gracefully when absent.

audits.summary: MeetingScribe's MCP server is frozen on the Nov-2024 spec (protocolVersion 2024-11-05, tools-only, stdio-only) while the 2026 market has commoditized "local + MCP" (Hyprnote, Meetily, Anarlog all ship local Whisper+Ollama+MCP) and moved up-stack to proactive agents (Granola's "Prep next meeting" + cited agentic chat) and the modern MCP surface (resources, prompts, HTTP, outputSchema). MeetingScribe's real, undefended moat is its relationship-graph + encounter history + in-app agentic tool-loop, none of which competitors have — but it under-exposes them and has no RAG/semantic layer, no proactive orchestration, and no voice. The highest-leverage 2026 bets turn the unique vault data into proactive, agentic, modern-MCP experiences that no Granola/Dex/Screenpipe can replicate.

recommendations.title: Modernize MCP server to the 2025-06-18 spec (resources + prompts + outputSchema + title)
recommendations.description: Sources/MeetingScribeMCP/main.swift still reports protocolVersion 2024-11-05 (line 1844) and capabilities {tools:{}} only (line 1845). The handle() switch (line 1861) has no resources/list, resources/read, prompts/list, or prompts/get cases. Bump to 2025-06-18; declare {tools, resources, prompts} capabilities; add a `title` and `outputSchema` to each of the 24 tool defs (esp. get_coaching_context at line 930 and list_encounters at 883). This is the foundation other MCP recs build on.
recommendations.userValue: Claude Desktop shows human-readable tool titles instead of list_overdue_check_ins, parses structured results deterministically (no hallucinated healthScore field), and can read proactive resources at session start. Brings the server level with every A-grade server in the 30K-server ecosystem.
recommendations.noveltyNote: Reinforces prior C5-2/C5-3 (outputSchema/title) but the load-bearing net-new finding is that the server is still pinned to the original 2024-11-05 spec and declares tools-only — none of the resources/prompts/HTTP work from prior plans has shipped. This is the enabling refactor, not just polish.

recommendations.title: MCP resources: live relationship-brief injected at session start
recommendations.description: Add a `resources/list` + `resources/read` handler to main.swift exposing relationship://brief (markdown: people overdue per cadence, birthdays next 30d, action items due this week, last 3 encounters) and relationship://privacy-notice. The data already exists via the same code paths that back tool_listOverdueCheckIns and tool_getCoachingContext. Claude reads the resource before the user types anything.
recommendations.userValue: Claude opens the session with 'Three people need attention: your sister (42d), Marcus (18d); Priya's birthday is in 6 days' with zero prompting. This is the 'proactive coach' experience — Granola/Dex/Screenpipe all require the user to ask a question first.
recommendations.noveltyNote: Confirms prior C4-1/C5-6 — still the single highest-strategic-value unbuilt item and no competitor occupies the proactive-context-injection niche. Flagged to not lose it.

recommendations.title: MCP prompts: relationship-coaching slash-commands in Claude Desktop
recommendations.description: Declare prompts capability and ship 3 prompts in main.swift: weekly_partner_brief(personId) (Gottman framing), reconnect_coaching(personId) for overdue people (NVC/love-language), post_encounter_reflection(personId, encounterId). prompts/get assembles live encounter/cadence data into a multi-message PromptMessage array. Appears as /-commands in Claude Desktop's palette.
recommendations.userValue: User types /weekly in Claude Desktop, picks their partner, and gets a framework-grounded briefing built from their real encounter history — the highest-visibility surface in the Claude UI, owned by nobody in the 30K-server ecosystem.
recommendations.noveltyNote: From prior C5-1; still uncontested and depends on the spec bump above. Endorsed.

recommendations.title: Fix coaching-framework coverage: only 2 of 7 relationship types get real guidance
recommendations.description: tool_getCoachingContext (main.swift ~1690-1709) only assigns a real framework for romantic_partner (Gottman, line 1695) and family_member (NVC, line 1696); friend, colleague, acquaintance and the rest fall through to the generic 'Active listening and consistent follow-through' default (line 1698). Add specific framework text per type. This is the server's headline differentiator returning worse-than-baseline output for the majority of relationships.
recommendations.userValue: Asking Claude to coach you on a friend or colleague returns substantive, type-specific guidance instead of a generic platitude — making the 'relationship coaching MCP' claim true for everyone, not just partners.
recommendations.noveltyNote: Prior C5-8 flagged this at ~43% fallback; I confirmed in source it's actually worse (only 2 explicit cases, ~5 of 7 types fall through). Must ship before any registry submission.

recommendations.title: RAG-over-vault: local embedding index + semantic_search MCP tool and in-app tool
recommendations.description: There is no semantic/vector layer anywhere — WorkspaceIndex.swift:106 search() is keyword/FTS5 only, and OllamaService uses /api/generate exclusively (no /api/embeddings). Build a local embedding index (Ollama nomic-embed-text or Apple NL embeddings) over transcripts, summaries, person bios/memories, and encounter notes; persist vectors in the vault. Expose as a semantic_search tool in BOTH the MCP server and the in-app ChatTools (Sources/MeetingScribe/Chat/), plus a search_across_everything unified tool.
recommendations.userValue: 'What did I discuss with Jordan about the Stripe migration?' returns the right passages by meaning, not keyword overlap — across meetings, people, voice notes, and encounters in one call. Granola's killer MCP feature is meeting search; this extends it across the richer vault and stays 100% local.
recommendations.noveltyNote: Net-new. Prior C4-3 proposed a unified search tool but assumed FTS5; the deeper finding is there is zero semantic retrieval in the codebase. RAG-over-vault is the 2026 second-brain standard and is entirely absent.

recommendations.title: Post-meeting agentic pipeline: enrich people, log encounters, and draft follow-ups automatically
recommendations.description: MeetingPipelineController.finalize() (ScribeCore, line 52) currently does summarize (Ollama) + regex ActionItemExtractor.extract (line 120). Add an agentic post-processing stage that runs an Ollama tool-loop (the same machinery as Sources/MeetingScribe/Chat/) to: match attendees to existing People, auto-log an encounter (log_encounter exists at main.swift:895 but is only Claude-invoked), append a per-person memory of what was discussed, and draft follow-up messages. No automation/webhook/rules engine exists today (grep confirms 'trigger/workflow' hits are unrelated UI).
recommendations.userValue: Every recorded meeting silently updates the relationship graph — encounters logged, people enriched, follow-ups drafted — so the second-brain stays current without the user manually invoking Claude. Turns passive transcription into an active CRM that maintains itself.
recommendations.noveltyNote: Net-new. Prior plans treat log_encounter as a Claude-triggered write tool; nobody proposed wiring the app's own agentic loop into the post-meeting pipeline. Closes the loop between the notetaker half and the second-brain half — the app's core thesis.

recommendations.title: Proactive pre-meeting brief from calendar + relationship graph (Granola 'Prep next meeting' parity, deepened)
recommendations.description: PreMeetingBriefView.swift is manual (user taps an upcoming event; computeBrief at line 160). Make it proactive: a scheduled job (the env already has cron/scheduling infra) fires N minutes before a calendar event, resolves attendees to People, and pushes a notification (NotificationManager.swift exists) with a one-tap brief: last 3 meetings with them, open action items owed to/by them, days since last encounter, birthday proximity. Also expose as an MCP get_meeting_prep(eventId) tool/resource.
recommendations.userValue: You get 'You meet Priya in 15 min — you owe her the Q3 doc, last spoke 18 days ago, her birthday is Friday' without opening the app. Granola shipped 'Prep next meeting'; MeetingScribe can beat it because it has the relationship graph Granola lacks.
recommendations.noveltyNote: Net-new framing: a manual PreMeetingBriefView already exists, but making it PROACTIVE + notification-driven + MCP-exposed (matching Granola's confirmed 2026 'Prep next meeting' feature) is not in prior plans.

recommendations.title: Streamable HTTP transport so Claude.ai web and agent platforms can reach the vault
recommendations.description: main.swift is stdio-only (readLine loop at line 1891). Add an optional Streamable HTTP transport (launch flag) bound to localhost with a token, sharing the same handle() dispatcher. The Nov-2025 spec made Streamable HTTP the standard; Granola/Dex are HTTP-based and reachable from Claude.ai web, while stdio-only servers are locked to Claude Desktop.
recommendations.userValue: Query your meetings and people from Claude.ai in a browser, ChatGPT, Cursor, or any agent runtime — not just the Claude Desktop app — while data still never leaves localhost.
recommendations.noveltyNote: From prior C4-4; still unbuilt and increasingly load-bearing as Claude.ai's web MCP support matures. Lower urgency than resources/prompts but required for cross-client reach.

recommendations.title: Privacy-grade tool annotations: mark intimate encounter/coaching output audience:[user]
recommendations.description: Add 2025-06-18 annotations to tool result content in main.swift for encounter notes and coaching analyses: {annotations:{audience:["user"], priority:0.9}} instead of the default assistant+user. Intimate notes about partners/family conflict are surfaced for the user's reflection rather than silently re-fed into model context. No personal-CRM or coaching server in the ecosystem does this.
recommendations.userValue: A concrete, machine-readable privacy guarantee — 'your most sensitive relationship notes are marked for your eyes, not the model's context' — that becomes a marketing claim no cloud competitor (Granola, Dex) can match.
recommendations.noveltyNote: From prior C5-4; endorsed as a first-mover trust differentiator. Pairs with the local-first positioning that is now table stakes but still under-stated.

recommendations.title: Voice agent: talk to your vault hands-free (local STT in, local TTS out)
recommendations.description: The app already ships local Whisper (ScribeCore/Transcription) and an agentic ChatSession (Sources/MeetingScribe/Chat/) but has no voice interface (grep for TTS/AVSpeech/wake found only unrelated UI). Add a push-to-talk voice loop: Whisper transcribes the question, ChatSession runs the tool-loop against the vault, AVSpeechSynthesizer (or a local TTS) reads the answer. Surface from MenuBarView/FloatingOverlay.
recommendations.userValue: 'Hey, who do I need to check in with this week?' answered aloud while you make coffee — a fully-local voice second-brain. Voice agents are the 2026 frontier and competitors with the same local stack (Hyprnote, Meetily) ship none.
recommendations.noveltyNote: Net-new. The two halves needed (local STT + agentic loop) already exist in-repo; only the voice I/O wrapper is missing. No prior audit doc proposes a voice interface.

recommendations.title: Scheduled relationship-coach agent: weekly digest + nudges via the harness cron
recommendations.description: Stand up a recurring local agent (cron/scheduling infra is present in the environment) that weekly runs the Chat tool-loop to compute overdue check-ins, birthday lookaheads, and stale-action-item triage, then writes a WeeklyRecap (WeeklyRecap.swift exists) and fires NotificationManager nudges. Optionally let it auto-attach its analysis to People via attach_note_to_person (already at main.swift:941).
recommendations.userValue: The coach works while you sleep: Monday morning you wake to 'Reconnect with 3 people this week, 2 birthdays coming, 4 tasks went stale' — proactive accountability no cold-start coaching app (CoupleWork, Ember) can offer.
recommendations.noveltyNote: Net-new: prior docs proposed nudge TOOLS Claude can call; this proposes a scheduled autonomous agent that runs them on a cadence and produces artifacts unprompted.

recommendations.title: Publish MeetingScribeMCP to Glama / mcpservers.org / GitHub MCP registry
recommendations.description: mcp-registry.json exists in the repo but the server is not indexed in any registry (Glama lists ~30K servers; competing nxt3d/mcp-crm and Coach AI are indexed, MeetingScribe is not). Tighten the description to lead with the coaching/relationship-graph differentiation, add category 'personal-context' + capabilities ['tools','resources','prompts'], and submit. Gate submission on the framework-coverage fix above.
recommendations.userValue: Organic discovery: every Claude power user searching for a personal-data MCP server finds MeetingScribe instead of a grade-C CRM clone. Zero-code distribution channel currently producing zero traffic.
recommendations.noveltyNote: From prior C4-6/C5-5/C5-7; confirmed still not done (no published listing) and a 30-minute distribution unlock. Sequenced after the coverage fix.

recommendations.title: Connect-the-app-agent to its own MCP server (and other servers) for a true agent hub
recommendations.description: The in-app ChatSession (Sources/MeetingScribe/Chat/) runs an Ollama/Anthropic tool-loop against hand-written Swift ChatTools, while MeetingScribeMCP + NotionMCP expose a parallel, separately-maintained tool surface — two implementations that drift (e.g. coaching framework logic lives only in the MCP). Add a generic MCP client to ChatSession so the in-app agent calls MCP servers (its own + Notion + user-added) over stdio, unifying the tool surface and letting users plug in arbitrary MCP servers.
recommendations.userValue: One agent, one tool catalog: the in-app Chat gains every MCP capability automatically (no more drift), and users can connect Gmail/Calendar/Linear/any MCP server to their local coach — positioning MeetingScribe as a local agent hub, not just a notetaker.
recommendations.noveltyNote: Net-new and architectural: prior plans never noticed the in-app ChatTools and the MCP server are duplicate tool surfaces that drift. Collapsing them into one MCP-client-driven agent is the ambitious 2026 bet that makes the app a platform.
